preamble: The shift from a postalveolar affricate [tʃ] to an alveolar affricate [ts] is a form of de-palatalization or fronting. This often occurs as an intermediate step in the simplification of affricates toward sibilant fricatives.

examples.note: Historical development where [tʃ] fronted to [ts] before eventually losing the stop component.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
